meta.next_session: 2026-06-30 10:45 AM PDT (Midday Check) — LAB stop/gap scan + final intraday entry window.

thoughts.label: SAFE MODE AVOIDED — ENGINE FIRED MANUALLY
thoughts.text: The 5:50 AM scheduled Signal Engine run did not fire, so the matrix was ~13.7h stale at session start. Per the new v1.8 rule, CLU manually triggered the Signal Engine workflow (run 28455643053), which completed in ~40s and refreshed signal_matrix.json to computed_at 2026-06-30T15:23:12Z (<2 min old). SAFE MODE was NOT entered — this ran as a normal session with new entries allowed.

thoughts.label: RECONCILED + GAP CHECK CLEAR
thoughts.text: Broker holdings matched memory (6 positions at entry). Live gap/stop check CLEAR: INTC $140.29 (surging +9.4% from entry, far above its $112.06 stop), ARKK $80.21, NVDA $197.83 — all above stops. CORE carries no stops (§13).

thoughts.label: DRIP SEEDED — JEPQ BUY $36
thoughts.text: DRIP was the most-underweight tier (0% vs 30%, -30pp) and the top capital priority. With SAFE MODE lifted, the covered-call path cleared the §15.3 screen (yield 10.0% > 1.5%; payout/streak N/A by rule), so the idle $36 cash was deployed into JEPQ (Nasdaq covered-call, fits the tech tilt), capturing tomorrow's 2026-07-01 ex-dividend. No stop placed (§15.4). DRIP now 23.6%.

thoughts.label: BOOK NOW 7 POSITIONS ACROSS ALL 3 TIERS
thoughts.text: Post-trade allocation: LAB 25.9% (INTC/ARKK/NVDA, +5.9pp over the 20% cap — dilutes via base growth, no force-sell §17.3), CORE 50.2% (on target), DRIP 23.6% (JEPQ, up from 0%, now only -6.4pp under). Cash fully deployed at $0.37. The -30pp DRIP gap that dominated prior sessions is largely closed.

thoughts.label: ENGINE STILL MISSING SCHEDULED RUNS
thoughts.text: The morning auto-run failed again (the manual trigger covered it this session). The v1.8 manual-trigger rule is a mitigation, not a fix — the GitHub Action cron/runner still needs investigation so the engine fires on schedule. LAB also remains over its 20% staging cap; no LAB adds authorized regardless.

learned_patterns.pattern: When the scheduled Signal Engine run misses, CLU can fire the workflow manually (workflow_dispatch) and get a fresh matrix in ~40s — so a missed cron no longer forces a frozen SAFE-MODE session. The session ran normally and executed a trade. SAFE MODE is now the fallback only when the manual run itself cannot produce fresh data.

The dividend-engine data gap (streak=0, payout=null) blocks only the dividend-growth names (SCHD/DGRO/VIG/VYM, which need streak>=5). Covered-call ETFs (JEPI/JEPQ) have streak/payout N/A by §15.3, so they pass on yield alone — making them the entryable DRIP path while the engine is incomplete.

LAB at 25.9% > 20% cap blocks new single-stock entries even on a passing gate (MU 4/5); §17.3 forbids force-selling to rebalance. Idle cash instead routes to the most-underweight tier (DRIP), which is exactly what closed the -30pp DRIP gap this session.

RXRX fell from 3/5 to 2/5 as its run-up pushed RSI to 67.9 (>65) and Z to 1.65 (>1.5). Gate status is a fast-moving target on momentum names — re-check every session; do not carry a stale 'ready' flag.
INTC gapped from $130.50 premarket to $140.29 intraday (+6.5%), nearing its +15% SOXX-migration trigger ($147.44). Holding the runner; migration logic will act if/when the trigger hits.
